backfill_note: The blog post shipped 2026-07-09; this run directory was authored on 2026-07-20 to make its firsthand claims reproducible. The original ad-hoc harness was not preserved, but every fixture and command was recorded verbatim in the workspace firsthand-benchmark.md, so the harness here is a faithful reconstruction. It was FRESHLY EXECUTED against astro@7.0.6 on 2026-07-20 (not a paper backfill) — probe-result.json is that execution's real output.

environment.runner: bash run.sh -> npm install + astro build/dev
environment.note: This is a compiler-behavior and CLI-lifecycle check, not a timing benchmark. Build/install times are machine-dependent and are intentionally NOT part of the evidence. Only deterministic pass/fail observations (whitespace collapse, hard compiler error, GFM built-ins, dev-server lifecycle) are used.

subject.name: astro
subject.note: Astro 7 replaced the old Go-based compiler with a Rust one and moved the build onto Vite 8 / Rolldown. Version pinned so behavior is reproducible; astro@7.1.x exists at authoring time but the post measured 7.0.6.

baseline.name: astro 6 (Go compiler)
baseline.note: The 'old behavior' contrasts (whitespace preserved, unterminated tags silently auto-closed) are cited from the migration guide / changelog, NOT executed here — Astro 6 was not installed. Only the Astro 7 side is measured firsthand.

tool.name: astro-cli + shell
tool.harness: run.sh (scaffolds project, drops fixtures/, builds, drives dev server)

raw_artifacts.retention_policy: none discarded — evidence is small and committed
raw_artifacts.note: The work/ scaffold (node_modules, dist) is regenerated by run.sh and gitignored. The deterministic evidence — probe-result.json — is committed. No large raw artifacts.
